cards.text: To output a single character to cout, use {{c1::std::cout.put(ch)}} or {{c2::std::cout << ch}}.
To set the output field width to 10 characters, use {{c1::std::cout << std::setw(10)}}. This requires {{c2::#include <iomanip>}}.

cards.front: What is the difference between std::setw() and most other manipulators?
cards.back: std::setw() only affects the next output operation, while most other manipulators (like std::setprecision) remain in effect until changed.

cards.text: To set the fill character for padding to '*', use {{c1::std::cout.fill('*')}} or {{c2::std::cout << std::setfill('*')}}.
To set floating-point precision to 4 digits, use {{c1::std::cout << std::setprecision(4)}} or {{c2::std::cout.precision(4)}}.

cards.question: What does this code output?
cards.explanation: By default, setprecision() sets the total number of significant digits (not decimal places). Use std::fixed to make it represent decimal places.

cards.text: To display numbers in hexadecimal, use {{c1::std::cout << std::hex}}. To return to decimal, use {{c2::std::cout << std::dec}}.
To print booleans as "true"/"false" instead of 1/0, use {{c1::std::cout << std::boolalpha}}.

cards.front: What does std::fixed do when used with setprecision()?
cards.back: It makes setprecision() control the number of decimal places (after the decimal point) instead of total significant digits.

cards.text: The three alignment manipulators are {{c1::std::left}} (left-align), {{c2::std::right}} (right-align), and {{c3::std::internal}} (align sign/base prefix).

cards.concept: Using iomanip manipulators vs member functions
cards.explanation: Manipulators (from <iomanip>) can be chained with << for cleaner code, while member functions require separate statements. Both achieve the same result, but manipulators are preferred for readability.
examples: First approach is more concise and readable

cards.question: What does this code output?
cards.answer: "   Hi    !" (Hi right-aligned in 5 chars, ! right-aligned in 5 chars)
cards.explanation: setw() must be set before each output because it only affects the next insertion. Default alignment is right.
